questions.question: What is the most common complication of type 2 diabetes mellitus?
questions.explanation: Diabetic retinopathy is the most common microvascular complication in type 2 diabetes mellitus, affecting more than 60% of patients after 20 years of evolution.

questions.question: Which acute complication is characterized by hyperglycemia and ketonemia?
questions.explanation: Diabetic ketoacidosis is an acute complication of diabetes characterized by hyperglycemia, ketonemia and metabolic acidosis, typical of type 1 diabetes but also possible in type 2.

questions.question: What is the most feared complication of systemic arterial hypertension?
questions.explanation: Stroke is the most feared complication of high blood pressure, being the main cause of disability and premature death in these patients.

questions.question: What surgical complication is associated with splenic rupture?
questions.explanation: Splenic rupture produces massive intra-abdominal hemorrhage that leads to hypovolemic shock, being a surgical emergency that requires urgent splenectomy.

questions.question: What is the most serious complication of untreated acute appendicitis?
questions.explanation: Generalized peritonitis is the most serious complication of acute appendicitis, resulting from appendiceal perforation, with high mortality if surgical intervention is not performed.

questions.question: What pulmonary complication is common in post-abdominal surgery patients?
questions.explanation: Basal atelectasis is the most common pulmonary complication after abdominal surgery, secondary to hypoventilation due to pain and decreased diaphragmatic mobility.
